direction.current_focus: Finish the world foundation through small threshold-hub-led slices so the first canon content can be written with a clearer social and geographic frame.
direction.why_now: The outward route shape is now clear enough that the next useful decision is one grounded local institution inside the hub instead of more broad world expansion.

items.title: Clean up duplicated continuity loading, stale helper surfaces, and drift between safety checks.
items.why: The rebuild stays trustworthy only if continuity wiring, safety checks, and entry points stay singular and easy to audit.
items.done_when: The builder-continuity prompt no longer double-loads Hermes memory, the safety checks match across loaders, stale transition files are gone, and helper scripts are portable.

items.title: Finish exact Hermes builder-memory adoption and remove any remaining fake builder-memory sources.
items.why: Builder continuity has to be exact and singular before the separate world systems are designed.
items.done_when: The vendored Hermes memory module is the only builder-memory implementation and the tests stay clean.

items.title: Keep one simple todo board that stays truthful as future slices land.
items.why: Planning drift is still how the repo gets sloppy again, even after the rebuild is cleaner.
items.done_when: After each clean stop, the board still has one active slice, a clear next horizon, and a clear later horizon without hidden thread context.

items.title: Define the canon engine boundary so world truth stays separate from operator memory and runtime beliefs.
items.why: World continuity is still unsolved until canon has its own home.
items.done_when: Canon facts, approvals, and runtime beliefs have separate boundaries.

items.title: Build the simulation runtime that reads canon without overwriting it.
items.why: The living world should emerge from canon instead of replacing it.
items.done_when: Runtime state can evolve scene by scene while canon stays authoritative.

items.title: Give the threshold hub a clean first shape: public image, internal faces, social pressures, and one local anchor.
items.why: The threshold hub is the best first concrete place for showing the transition from center life to frontier life without naming the whole outer world too early.
items.done_when: The hub reads like a place instead of a placeholder and still stays clearly in working-design territory.

items.title: Define what the threshold hub points toward first beyond itself.
items.why: The next clean decision is not a full region map but the first outward direction that makes departures from the hub feel pointed instead of abstract.
items.done_when: The foundation names the first kind of place, route, or frontier contrast people imagine when they leave the hub, without locking the whole outer world too early.

items.title: Define one local institution that embodies the struggle between sponsor legitimacy and lived frontier competence.
items.why: A single institution can make the hub's social conflict feel grounded without forcing full political systems too early.
items.done_when: One hub institution clearly shows how inland authority and real outward credibility collide.

items.title: Define who stays in the threshold hub and what a non-expedition future there can look like.
items.why: The setting needs slower local futures to feel alive, not like failure states beside the frontier.
items.done_when: The hub has a clear sense of ordinary belonging, staying, romance, work, and local adulthood beyond departure culture.

items.title: Turn the world foundation into the first small canon content slice.
items.why: Once the foundation is clear, the next step is approved world facts, not simulation behavior.
items.done_when: The first canon entities, places, groups, rules, and timeline anchors are drafted in a reviewable shape.

items.title: Define orchestrator model lanes and provider boundaries before runtime work begins.
items.why: The system should stay portable across providers and models. Runtime-social work may want a different lane than build, canon, or coding work.
items.done_when: The repo has a small architecture note describing task lanes, provider boundaries, and swappable model choices without coupling world files to one backend.

items.title: Build the player-facing layer for entering the living world.
items.why: The user insertion layer only makes sense after canon and simulation are stable.
items.done_when: The system can place the user into a persistent scene without confusing builder state with world state.
